g.text: bind :: Prob a -> (a -> Prob b) -> Prob b

div.p: A Prob a value is a list of (outcome, probability) pairs. The probabilities should sum to 1.
bind dist f = [(b, pa*pb) | (a,pa) <- dist, (b,pb) <- f a]
This is the law of total probability / conditional probability: P(B = b) = sum over a of P(B = b | A = a) * P(A = a)

g.title: For each (a, pa) in dist, apply f to get a Prob b. Each (b, pb) gets weight pa * pb. Cartesian product weighted by joint probability.
Assign equal probability 1/n to each element.
Sum the probabilities of all outcomes where pred holds.
